source_jobs.title: Technical Program Manager, Payments & Billing
source_jobs.description:
TECHNICAL PROGRAM MANAGER, PAYMENTS & BILLING | ACORNS

At Acorns, our mission is to look after the financial best interests of the up-and-coming, beginning with the empowering, proud step of micro-investing. How do we accomplish our mission? Our values: Lead With Heart, Make Bold Decisions, Always Build Trust, Never Stop Growing, and Find a Way. These values guide us, and our mission drives us. Come join us and help deliver financial wellness for the whole family, putting the tools of wealth-making into everyone’s hands.



Acorns is seeking a Technical Program Manager to lead the evolution of our Payments, Billing, and Account Linking infrastructure. In this role, you will be the architect and operator of the payment, point of sale, and money movement systems for millions of customers. You will focus on identifying improvements in the current systems and own the delivery of new product launches to provide the highest quality customer experience while maintaining a robust financial posture and scaling our systems for global use.



Acorns is a remote-first organization, offering the flexibility to work remotely while providing optional access to office space in Irvine, CA.




WHAT YOU WILL DO AT ACORNS:

 - Infrastructure Strategy: Collaborate with Engineering, Finance, and Data Science to define and execute the payments and billing roadmap, ensuring Acorns’ management of funding sources and account linking is able to scale and our billing platform is dynamic to enable net new product opportunities.

 - Seamless Account Linking: Identify areas of opportunity and drive the delivery of improvements for bank account connectivity, improving the customer experience across account linking and verification experiences and building sophisticated monitoring to prevent blind spots and future issues.

 - Billing and Payment Excellence: Develop the strategy for how Acorns will leverage technologies like Recurly, Apple Pay, Google Pay, debit card providers, and ACH to reduce friction for our customers and implement the proper controls to prevent future billing concerns, optimizing the customer experience and delivery high quality product improvements.

 - Frictionless Money Movement: Expand Acorns ecosystem beyond Apple Pay and Google Pay to include future integrations like Venmo, Zelle, and other emerging rails, optimizing both the backend architecture and the customer experience.

 - Operational Efficiency: Treat operations as a customer by driving workflow strategies and tools that help scale the internal team's value and efficiency in managing payment exceptions and billing inquiries.

 - Vendor & Partner Management: Lead high-stakes relationships and technical integrations with processors, bank SaaS providers, payment vendors, and merchants.
   


WHAT YOU WILL BRING TO ACORNS:

 - Technical Product Mastery: 5+ years of Technical Program Management experience in Fintech, with a proven track record of running complex projects that balance technical constraints with customer needs.

 - Payments Expertise: Strong foundational knowledge of the mechanics of apple pay, google pay, credit/debit cards, prepaid cards, and the nuances of working with payment processors, point of sale systems, and banking institutions.

 - Technical Savvy: An understanding of APIs, SQL, and the mechanics of payment processing and account linking (e.g., Plaid).

 - Account Connectivity Knowledge: Deep understanding of account connectivity (e.g., Plaid, manual linking, and automated verification) and how to optimize these for conversion and security.

 - Customer Obsession: A mindset that prioritizes both the Acorns customer and our internal operations team, with the ability to build a seamless payment platform that reflects our brand.

 - Scientific Approach: Expertise in data analysis and the ability to articulate using data the tradeoff between enhancing security postures against adding friction to the customer experience.

 - Hunger to deliver game-changing

Sr. Software Engineer (AI Team) | Acorns

Acorns is seeking a Senior Software Engineer to join our AI team! Are you passionate about cutting-edge AI technologies? Do you thrive in an environment where your skills can redefine customer experiences and positively impact lives? Join Acorns as we build the future of Fintech with a customer service virtual agent that’s as empowering as our mission.



You will harness the power of AI to design a virtual agent that redefines customer support by providing personalized, real-time assistance. You will utilize proprietary data to create intelligent, empathetic solutions that empower our users to make informed financial decisions. Playing a pivotal role, you'll help shape a product that embodies our core values of benevolence and courage, transforming customer service into an uplifting and impactful experience.



Acorns is a remote-first organization, offering the flexibility to work remotely while providing optional access to office space in Irvine, CA.



What you will do at Acorns:

 - Understand Acorns’ mission, vision, strategic objectives, and fintech industry knowledge while building relationships with team members, stakeholders, and cross-functional teams.

 - Review and analyze proprietary datasets, existing Vector database architecture, pipelines, and tools to identify gaps and propose improvements.

 - Contribute to key customer-facing projects, debugging and enhancing models, scaling pipelines, and delivering the virtual support bot MVP to production.

 - Collaborate with cross-functional teams to align AI efforts with business goals, mentor junior team members, and drive innovation through LLM capabilities.

 - Optimize data pipelines, establish scalable monitoring processes, and develop a roadmap for innovation aligned with fintech objectives.



What you will bring to Acorns:

 - Bachelor’s or Master’s Degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, Data Science, Statistics, or a STEM-related field

 - 5+ years of total software engineering experience

 - 3+ years of experience in artificial intelligence, data science, or machine learning engineering

 - 3+ years of experience with modern cloud computing technologies, such as AWS, Bedrock, Sagemaker, Databricks, or Kubernetes

 - Foundational knowledge with Generative AI, Transformer, fine tuning LLM, Search ranking, RAG improvement, and LLM Agent

 - Experience tuning neural networks on custom datasets.

 - Experience with Huggingface, LangChain, chatbot development

 - Proficiency in programming languages and analytical tools, such as Python or PyTorch, Pandas, NumPy, SciKit-Learn, Matplotlib

 - Hunger to deliver game-changing products

 - Exceptional drive and precision in delivery

 - A belief that your work is tied to your life's mission

 - Optimistic about the potential of societal change



You are not expected to have experience with all listed requirements. If you

Sr. Recruiter | Acorns



Every strong team starts with a thoughtful hire. Someone who brings the skills, perspective, and heart to help build something greater. At Acorns, our recruiters make that possible by connecting mission-driven people to work that matters.



We’re looking for a Sr. Technical Recruiter to help us grow the teams building the future of financial wellness, at both Acorns and GoHenry. You’ll lead full-cycle recruiting efforts while building scalable hiring systems that help us find and support top talent.



In this role, you’ll partner closely with hiring managers and cross-functional leaders to deepen our expertise in how we source, evaluate, and hire candidates. From day one, you’ll help shape how we find, hire, and support the people behind our mission, across two companies working together to help millions grow financial wellness for themselves and their families.



Acorns is a remote-first organization, offering the flexibility to work remotely while providing optional access to office space in Irvine, CA.



What you will do at Acorns:

 - Partner deeply with leadership to design and deliver hiring strategies that meet business needs while also attracting and retaining world-class talent at scale, across all our geographies.

 - Lead full-cycle recruiting for roles, ensuring a seamless, values-aligned candidate experience from outreach to offer.

 - Develop a deep understanding of roles for both Acorns and GoHenry.

 - Relentlessly source and build pipelines of uniquely talented, diverse candidates using creative and targeted outreach strategies.

 - Evaluate and enhance technical recruiting operations, including interview structure, candidate evaluation tools, and technical interview bank to drive strong hiring decisions

 - Serve as a thought partner to leadership by connecting recruiting insights to broader business outcomes.

 - Mentor and guide team members, helping to raise the bar for hiring across the organization.

 - Collaborate cross-functionally with HR and hiring teams to build a strong employer brand and create scalable hiring programs.



What you will bring to Acorns:

 - 6+ years of technical or non-technical recruiting experience

 - Proven track record to deliver high-impact hires across all technical disciplines in fast-paced, high-growth, or mission-driven companies.

 - Deep expertise in building and maintaining networks with emerging technical talent

 - Deep familiarity with hiring software engineers, security engineers, designers, and product managers

 - Insight into ML, AI, and LLM-related technical trends and talent landscapes

 - Strong relationship management skills, with the ability to influence and partner effectively at the highest levels of the organization.

 - Operational rigor with a proactive, solution-oriented mindset

 - Hunger to deliver game-changing products

 - Exceptional drive and precision in delivery

 - A belief that your

GROWTH MARKETING, SR. ASSOCIATE - NEW CHANNELS | ACORNS

 

Acorns is looking for a curious, driven Growth Marketing Sr. Associate to join our team and help expand our paid acquisition channel mix. You will work closely with the Senior Paid Acquisition Manager to research, launch, and scale emerging platforms—from programmatic DSPs to community-based channels like Reddit—driving user growth across Acorns and Acorns Early.



This role carries real ownership. You’ll operate with meaningful day-to-day autonomy, exercise judgment within established frameworks, and produce analysis and recommendations that shape how we invest in new channels. You’ll collaborate cross-functionally with Analytics, Creative, and channel partners to bring experiments to life and move quickly from proof of concept to scaled channel.



Acorns is a remote-first organization, offering the flexibility to work remotely while providing optional access to office space in Irvine, CA.




WHAT YOU WILL DO

Channel Testing & Execution

 - Support the development and execution of a structured testing roadmap for new and emerging paid media channels, including programmatic display, Reddit, Snapchat, and alternative platforms.

 - Manage day-to-day campaign setup, trafficking, and pacing across new channel tests, exercising judgment on optimizations and escalating to the Senior Paid Acquisition Manager on larger strategic decisions.

 - Partner with the Creative team to brief and iterate on ad assets tailored to new platform environments.

Measurement & Reporting

 - Identify incrementality and lift study opportunities, collaborate with the Analytics team to design the test structure, and own the launch and ongoing performance monitoring of each experiment.

 - Track and report on key performance indicators for new channel initiatives, translating data into clear summaries for internal stakeholders.

 - Maintain organized records of test results, learnings, and optimization actions to build a shared knowledge base for the team.

Cross-Brand & Cross-Functional Collaboration

 - Support acquisition growth across our brand portfolio—Acorns, Acorns Early, and GoHenry—with a focus on US-based performance.

 - Work collaboratively with Analytics, Creative, and other marketing teammates to execute experiments efficiently and surface actionable insights.

 - Actively monitor the AdTech landscape—including gaming platforms (e.g., Roblox, Discord), community platforms, and emerging programmatic DSPs—and bring forward channel opportunities for the team to evaluate.




WHAT YOU WILL BRING

 - 3+ years of experience in performance marketing, paid media, or growth marketing with a BS/BA; or equivalent work experience.

 - Hands-on experience with at least one paid media platform (e.g., Meta, Snapchat, Reddit, The Trade Desk, or similar).

 - Familiarity with mobile measurement platforms (e.g., Adjust, AppsFlyer) and an understanding of how attribution f

Product Marketing, Manager II | Acorns



Acorns is seeking a Product Marketing Manager II to support the messaging, go-to-market strategy, and evergreen content programs that drive adoption across the Acorns and Acorns Early product portfolios. In this role, you will help develop product positioning frameworks, support key launches, and contribute to a robust asset library that enables consistent customer education. You will integrate modern AI tools into your daily workflow to drive efficiency and help evolve product marketing best practices across the team.



In this role, you will own the master messaging frameworks and lead high-priority product launches end-to-end. AI fluency is foundational to this role. You are expected to integrate modern AI tools into the daily practice of product marketing, build reusable workflows that elevate the standards of the broader team, and continuously raise the quality bar for how the function operates.



Acorns is a remote-first organization, offering the flexibility to work remotely while providing optional access to office space in Irvine, CA.



What you will do at Acorns:

 - Product Messaging & Storytelling: Support the development and maintenance of messaging frameworks that connect product capabilities to customer benefits across all touchpoints.

 - Go-To-Market Execution: Support end-to-end GTM execution for product launches, including creative briefing, channel coordination, and launch communications.

 - Voice of Customer Integration: Synthesize insights from research, customer feedback, and social sentiment into messaging recommendations to ensure we are speaking the customer's language.

 - Evergreen Content & Adoption: Maintain a library of evergreen assets—such as FAQs, value propositions, and app store creative—to improve product understanding and reduce trust barriers.

 - AI-Enabled Operations: Apply modern AI tools and workflows to improve the speed, quality, and consistency of product marketing deliverables.

 - Cross-Functional Collaboration: Partner closely with Product, Brand, Growth, Lifecycle, Research, and Compliance to execute initiatives that drive measurable customer adoption.



What you will bring to Acorns:

 - 5+ years of product marketing experience, with a track record of supporting product launches (Consumer, fintech, or subscription experience preferred).

 - Strong communication and storytelling skills, with the ability to translate technical features into clear customer value.

 - Proficiency in using AI tools to support messaging development, content creation, or research synthesis.

 - Proven ability to synthesize customer insights into actionable marketing recommendations.

 - Experience developing customer-facing marketing assets and supporting evergreen content systems.

 - Ability to collaborate effectively across diverse teams in a fast-paced, regulated environment.

 - Hunger to deliver game-chan

Programmatic Channel Sr. Associate | Acorns



Acorns is seeking a Senior Associate, Programmatic to support the execution, optimization, and day-to-day management of our programmatic advertising efforts. In this role, you will analyze performance data, implement campaign optimizations, and help ensure we are reaching customers at the right time with the right message while supporting Acorns’ mission of making financial wellness accessible to all.



Acorns is a remote-first organization, offering the flexibility to work remotely while providing optional access to office space in Irvine, CA.



What you will do at Acorns:

 - Support the execution of programmatic campaigns across DSPs to drive customer acquisition and engagement

 - Assist in managing the campaign lifecycle, including trafficking, pacing, monitoring performance, and resolving technical issues

 - Analyze performance data and implement optimizations across targeting, creative, and bidding to improve efficiency and ROI

 - Partner with Data, Analytics, and Creative teams to ensure campaigns align with broader marketing goals and brand standards

 - Contribute to reporting and insights by tracking performance and helping quantify the impact of programmatic on customer growth



What you will bring to Acorns:

 - Hands-on experience managing programmatic media buying across DSPs, including platforms such as The Trade Desk, along with PMPs and ad servers, with a proven track record of meeting performance expectations.

 - Strong analytical skills with the ability to interpret complex metrics into actionable campaign improvements.

 - Operational rigor and a proactive approach to solving campaign issues and navigating the evolving digital landscape.

 - Hunger to deliver game-changing products.

 - Exceptional drive and precision in delivery.

 - A belief that your work is tied to your life's mission.

 - Optimistic about the potential of societal change.

You are not expected to have experience with all listed requirements. If you feel passionate about Acorns' mission, vision, and values, please apply.

 

What we offer:

 - Competitive salary and stock options

 - A comprehensive benefits package for you and your family

 - Flexible work location, hours, and paid time off

 - 401(k) matching

 - Acorns Career Development Program (Ongoing training sessions, development plans, development check-ins, Cornerstone’s online training platform)

 - Roots Leadership Program for Emerging Leaders

 - Community week onsite gatherings and various virtual events

 - Talented and motivated team members who care deeply about one another, our mission, and our customers.

Financial Operations Associate | Acorns



Acorns is seeking a Financial Operations Associate to play a key role in the daily management and execution of our investment product operations, ensuring accuracy and efficiency in cash and security transfers, while maintaining meticulous documentation and compliance.



Acorns is a remote-first organization, offering the flexibility to work remotely while providing optional access to office space in Irvine, CA.



What you will do at Acorns:

 - You'll be instrumental in the smooth running of our financial operations, helping to look after the financial best interests of our up-and-coming customers.

 - Assist in the processing of the firm’s daily cash and security transfers related to the firm’s investment products.

 - Monitor customer cash balances on a daily and periodic basis to identify unusual fluctuations, deficits, or excess balances.

 - Gather essential data points from vendors to support daily operational tasks.

 - Collaborate with Operations, Finance, Compliance, and Risk teams to ensure accurate reporting and classification of customer funds

 - Improve and maintain documentation of internal control processes, ensuring clarity and compliance.

 - Collaborate closely with the Investment Operations team to identify, research, and resolve discrepancies and improve existing processes.

 - Contribute to special projects focused on continuous process improvements and operational efficiency.

 - Ensure WORM-compliance related to FinOp books and records.

 - Work with manager and team to establish priorities, including new features, infrastructure, and tool building.



What you will bring to Acorns:

 - We're looking for a detail-oriented, driven individual with a passion for financial technology and our mission.

 - 0 to 1 year+ of professional experience in a financial services, FinTech, or highly regulated operations role.

 - Educational background in Finance, Accounting, Business, or a related field.

 - Strong analytical skills and a meticulous attention to detail to ensure accuracy in all operational processes.

 - Ability to quickly learn and understand Broker-Dealer compliance requirements, such as WORM (Write Once, Read Many), and financial record-keeping.

 - A proactive approach to identifying potential issues and proposing solutions.

 - Excellent collaboration and communication skills to work effectively with cross-functional teams and external vendors.

 - Hunger to deliver game-changing products.

 - Exceptional drive and precision in delivery.

 - A belief that your work is tied to your life's mission.

 - Optimistic about the potential of societal change.

You are not expected to have experience with all listed requirements. If you feel passionate about Acorns' mission, vision, and values, please apply.

 - Competitive salary and stock options

source_jobs.title: Staff Product Manager, Conversational AI
source_jobs.description:
Staff Product Manager, Conversational AI | Acorns



Acorns is seeking a Staff Product Manager to lead the product strategy for Ask Acorns, our AI-powered assistant, across both the Acorns and Acorns Early brands. This is a high-impact, high-visibility role that sits at the center of how Acorns uses AI to advance financial wellness for everyday people and families.

Acorns is a remote-first organization, offering the flexibility to work remotely while providing optional access to office space in Irvine, CA.



What you will do at Acorns:

 - Lead Product Strategy: Define how Ask Acorns evolves as an intelligence layer across both the Acorns and Acorns Early brands over time

 - Own the Roadmap: Define and drive the Ask Acorns roadmap, partnering with product teams across Financial Planning, Financial Products, Families, and Growth to align priorities, surface dependencies, and ensure capabilities are built and sequenced in service of the broader product strategy

 - Shape the Experience: Partner with design to craft Ask Acorns' voice, tone, and persona, and how it handles the edges of what it can help with

 - Define Skills and Capabilities: Build the strategy for Ask Acorns' skills and capabilities, including memory and longitudinal understanding, proactivity, conversation threading, and the guardrails that govern experience and safety.

 - Set the Quality Bar: Partner with engineering to establish the quality and performance bar for Ask Acorns, including latency, reliability, and the infrastructure that connects Ask Acorns to the broader Acorns product ecosystem

 - Measure and Improve: Define success metrics and eval infrastructure, partner with research to build customer feedback loops, and use conversation transcripts and system instrumentation to detect issues and improve over time

 - Evolve How We Work: Drive new ways of working across teams to improve the quality and speed of AI product development
   
   

What you will bring to Acorns:

 - 10+ years of product management experience, with at least 2 years building and shipping conversational AI products

 - Ability to define a clear product strategy and translate it into a roadmap that balances near-term execution with strategic bets, moving quickly and adapting as the AI landscape evolves

 - Heart-led and customer-obsessed, bringing genuine empathy to the work and a deep curiosity about the financial lives, hopes, and fears of everyday Americans

 - Bias for speed and action; able to make decisions with incomplete information and move quickly while maintaining the rigor a regulated environment requires

 - Deep understanding of conversational AI systems, including prompt engineering, memory, guardrails, and evals, with enough technical fluency to ask the right questions and make informed tradeoffs with engineering

 - Sharp data instincts: knowing which metrics matter, analyzing performance with depth, and using data to drive decisions and hold teams accountable

 - Strong executive presence and communication skills, including the ability to align teams, build organizational buy-in, and present effectively to senior leadership and the CEO

 - Hands-on with AI tools, using them actively to prototype ideas, stress-test assumptions, and accelerate the product development process

 - Hunger to deliver game-changing products

 - Exceptional drive and precision in delivery

 - A belief that your work is tied to your life's mission

 - Optimistic about the potential of societal change
   
   

You are not expected to have experience with all listed requirements. If you feel passionate about Acorns' mission, vision, and values, please apply.



What we offer:

 - Competitive salary and stock options

 - A comprehensive benefits package for you and your family

 - Flexible work location, hours, and paid time off

 - 401(k) matching

 - Monthly Acorns account contribution & GoHenry account for your family

 - Mindfulness and Financial Wellness resources, Headspace and Additi

SR. FINANCE ANALYST | ACORNS



Acorns is seeking an ambitious Sr. Finance Analyst to enable, inform, and improve strategic and financial decision-making across the company. This role will work directly with the VP of Accounting, Finance and Treasurer as well as teams throughout the organization, including product, engineering, marketing, legal, and operations.



Acorns is a remote-first organization, offering the flexibility to work remotely while providing optional access to office space in Irvine, CA and New York, NY.




WHAT YOU WILL DO AT ACORNS:

 - Introduce yourself to the organization and develop a deep understanding of the product, business model, and financial outlook.

 - Equip product managers and department leaders with monthly insights on business trends.

 - Support short- and long-term financial plans, assist in financial reporting and KPI variance analysis, and help drive key strategic initiatives.

 - Develop, maintain, and enhance corporate financial models, including annual budgets, forecasts, and consolidated cash flows.

 - Build KPI frameworks, analyze product line performance, and support high-impact decisions including go-to-market strategy, partnership negotiations, and potential M&A.

 - Create clear presentations to articulate findings to key stakeholders.




WHAT YOU WILL BRING TO ACORNS:

 - Bachelor’s Degree in Accounting, Economics, Finance, Business, or another quantitative field.

 - Two or more years of relevant industry experience with financial modeling (e.g., investment banking, private equity, corporate finance).

 - Highly analytical mindset with the ability to execute complex financial modeling, analyze raw data, and draw clear conclusions.

 - High degree of organization, efficiency, and exceptional attention to detail.

 - Strong written and verbal communication skills with the ability to collaborate effectively across cross-functional teams.

 - A team-player mindset with a desire to roll up your sleeves and be hands-on in an entrepreneurial, fast-paced environment.

 - Strong sense of ownership, pride of work, and the ability to self-start.

 - Highest ethical standards, integrity, authenticity, credibility, and character.

 - Hunger to deliver game-changing products.

 - Exceptional drive and precision in delivery.

 - A belief that your work is tied to your life's mission.

 - Optimistic about the potential of societal change.

 - A strong passion for the Acorns’ mission.



You are not expected to have experience with all listed requirements. If you feel passionate about Acorns' mission, vision, and values, please apply.

 - Flexible work location, hours, and paid time off.

Sr. Product Operations Specialist | Acorns



Acorns is seeking an ambitious, entrepreneurial, and highly organized Product Operations Specialist to work closely with our Head of Product. In this high-impact role, you will drive operational excellence, elevate our cross-functional delivery frameworks, and scale the tools that empower our product organization to build world-class financial wellness solutions. You are a fast learner who thrives in a dynamic environment, balancing strategic foresight with absolute precision in execution.



Acorns is a remote-first organization, offering the flexibility to work remotely while providing optional access to office space in Irvine, CA.



What you will do at Acorns:

 - Operations Leadership: Partner directly with the Head of Product to optimize organization efficiency, streamline internal workflows, support team leaders, and project-manage critical product operations initiatives.

 - Strategic Architecture & Roadmap Planning: Build and evolve the frameworks, tooling, and ways of working used for long-range strategic planning, roadmap prioritization and capacity planning. Own the administration and data integrity of the company’s unified roadmap, mapping product outcomes directly to business and financial metrics.

 - Delivery Integration & Continuous Improvement: Connect delivery planning across all functional teams (design, engineering, analytics, marketing, legal, support, etc.) to ensure seamless cross-organizational alignment, while continuously tracking execution to identify and eliminate operational bottlenecks and inefficiencies.

 - Executive Reporting & Routines: Lead core product operating routines, including the end-to-end orchestration, data aggregation, and materials preparation for Monthly Business Reviews (MBRs) and executive reporting.

 - Vendor & Budget Management: Manage the operational budget for product teams, including third-party service and software contracts.
   

What you will bring to Acorns:

 - Jira & JPD Mastery: Expert-level, hands-on experience architecture-building within Jira and Jira Product Discovery (JPD), with a proven track record of creating custom views, automations, workflows, and executive-level reports.

 - AI-Driven Workflows: Demonstrated proficiency using artificial intelligence (AI) tools to design, build, and optimize automated reporting and internal operational productivity tools.

 - Financial & Operational Literacy: Clear experience managing technology vendor relationships, tracking software budgets, and navigating corporate contract pipelines.

 - Cross-Functional Partnerships: Exceptional interpersonal skills with a natural ability to collaborate, communicate direction clearly, and bring fun to a highly collaborative, fast-moving environment.

 - Project Management Expertise: Strong foundational project management skills, with a proven ability to organize complex timelines, manage risk and dependencies, and coordinate communication across teams.

 - Core A

source_jobs.title: Data Analyst II
source_jobs.description:
AT ACORNS, OUR MISSION IS TO LOOK AFTER THE FINANCIAL BEST INTERESTS OF THE UP-AND-COMING, BEGINNING WITH THE EMPOWERING, PROUD STEP OF MICRO-INVESTING. HOW DO WE ACCOMPLISH OUR MISSION? OUR VALUES.



Lead With Heart - With compassion, integrity, and tenacity, inspire yourself and others to move past all previous thresholds of possibility



Make Bold Decisions - We are creating a new world. Be optimistic, adventurous, and courageous. Leap fearlessly into the future



Always Build Trust - Say what you mean, mean what you say, and do what you say you’re going to do. We are all owners. We are one team



Never Stop Growing - Change IS the constant. Stay hungry and curious. Be relentless in the pursuit of progress



Find a way - Never settle, no excuses, nothing is impossible. Just make it happen

Our values guide us, and our mission drives us. Come join us and help deliver financial wellness for the whole family, putting the tools of wealth-making into everyone’s hands.

 
 

Data Analyst II | Acorns

Acorns is seeking a Data Analyst II to own measurement, analyses, and experimentation across the customer journey, measuring campaign and experiment effectiveness, identifying new customer segments, uncovering opportunities for communication touchpoints that drive engagement, and evaluating what's working across product and lifecycle marketing. Every experiment you design, every audience you segment, every campaign you measure, and every insight you surface directly influences how customers start and engage with Acorns.



This is a strategic partner role, not a ticket-taker. You'll sit at the table with product managers, marketers, and engineers, advocating for measurement rigor, pushing back when experiment designs or attribution claims are flawed, and surfacing insights that change roadmaps and campaign strategies. We're looking for someone who has a point of view and isn't afraid to use it.



Acorns is a remote-first organization, offering the flexibility to work remotely while providing optional access to office space in Irvine, CA.

 
 


WHAT YOU WILL DO AT ACORNS:

 - Own experimentation and analyses end-to-end: Partner with product and marketing teams to design A/B tests, measure campaign incrementality, determine sample sizes and duration, analyze results with statistical rigor, and translate findings into decisions. You ensure we're actually learning, not just reporting.

 - Investigate when results don't add up: Dig into anomalies, validate tracking, segment results, and determine whether unexpected outcomes are signal or noise

 - Surface insights proactively: Monitor performance, campaign results, and engagement patterns to identify opportunities and flag issues before they escalate.

 - Connect metrics to customer outcomes: A statistically significant lift in click-through rate doesn't matter if it doesn't improve the customer experience or drive business value. You think about leading indicators, lagging indicators, and how to measure what actually matters.

 - Shape Priorities and Infrastructure: Work with cross-functional teams to establish priorities, including new infrastructure and tool building that improves the team's analytical capabilities and scales measurement across the organization.

 - Scale your impact with AI and automation: Use AI tools to accelerate analysis, generate code, and automate repetitive work. Contribute to the team's investment in agent-based workflows and AI-driven automation.

 
 


WHAT YOU WILL BRING TO ACORNS:

 - 5+ years of Analytics experience preferably supporting product or marketing.

 - Measurement and experimentation experience: You've designed experiments or measurement frameworks, calculated sample sizes, analyzed results with proper statistical methods, and communicated findings to non-technical stakeholders.

 - Strong statistics foundation: Hypothesis testing, p-values, confidence intervals, Type I and Type II errors, and statistical power.

 - Familiarity with mode

notes: Acorns is a fintech company with a mix of tech and non-tech roles. The company has a strong focus on AI and data analytics.
